rows.notes: Clear centered page year at top. No monetary amounts visible; handwriting is legible.

rows.notes: Clear heading under the year. No amounts in this header row.
Numbers '187' (pounds) and '10' (shillings) are clearly written and aligned in the pounds and shillings columns respectively; no pence shown.
Single clear number '50' aligned in the pounds column; shillings/pence blank.
Clear '37' in pounds column and '10' in shillings column; no pence.
Only a single '15' appears aligned with the shillings column (no pounds). Handwriting for the description is legible; alignment makes this read as 15s.
Clear '12' (pounds) and '10' (shillings) in their respective columns.
Description is slightly cramped but readable. Numbers '3' and '15' are aligned in pounds and shillings; handwriting of 'Michm' and the date is slightly ambiguous.
Repeat of earlier 'Subrector' entry; '37' and '10' are clearly aligned in pounds and shillings columns.
Single '15' aligned in shillings column (ditto 'Dean' with no pounds). Slight ink variation but alignment is consistent.
Three numbers '4' / '5' / '5' are visible and align with pounds / shillings / pence columns respectively; pence '5' is clear.
Clear '12' (pounds) and '10' (shillings) aligned in the columns; consistent with prior Junr. Bursar entries.
Description legible; '50' clearly in pounds column; shillings/pence blank.
Again clear '12' and '10' in pounds/shillings columns.
Consistent repeat entry; '37' (pounds) and '10' (shillings) are unambiguous.
Single '15' appears under shillings column (ditto). Slightly faded but alignment supports 15s.
Right-hand numbers look like '16' and '0' across pounds and shillings/pence; the ink is slightly blotched around this line making the zeros less crisp.

Small single digit '1' is visible near the pounds column but could alternatively be a shilling mark; chosen as 1 pound due to vertical alignment slightly left of shillings column. Description spelling uncertain ('Willert' / 'Willett').
Third occurrence of Subrector with clear '37' and '10' in pounds/shillings columns.
Name 'Sheppard' readable; '12' and '10' clearly aligned in pounds and shillings columns.
Short form 'Do' (ditto). Single '15' visible in the shillings column; ink slightly lighter but alignment supports 15s.

Three numerals read as '178 / 9 / 1' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Text is cursive and slightly compressed; chosen reading follows clear vertical alignment of the three numbers though the description abbreviation is uncertain.

Numerals appear as '56 / 16 / 6' in the three columns. The written note contains '£6.0.2' which seems at odds with the printed numerals; I recorded what is visible in the columns (56/16/6). Handwriting of the name 'Ninney' is moderately clear.

Column numerals read '137 / 8 / 7'. The written parenthetical amount '£8.5.8' in the description differs from the column numerals; captured the clear column values though the reason for the discrepancy is unclear from the page.

Description contains multiple figures (71.0.10 and 22.10) written inline; the columns show a clear '83' in the pounds column but shillings/pence are faint/obscured. I recorded '83' pounds only; the exact breakdown between the two amounts in the description is ambiguous.

Columns show '48 / 13 / 9'. The description contains '£6' which does not match column totals; recorded what is visible in the numeric columns. Ink is slightly blotched on this line causing moderate uncertainty.
Three clear numerals '5 / 1 / 6' align with pounds/shillings/pence. Description is legible and context (books for chapel) is clear.
Clear '14' and '5' in pounds and shillings columns respectively; no pence shown. Name is easy to read.
Numerals '8 / 18 / 6' are clearly aligned in the three currency columns; handwriting for 'Vincent' is legible.
Small numerals '1' and '7' visible in pounds and shillings columns; the place name 'Warboro.' is slightly abbreviated and the pence column is blank.
Final boxed/ruled total at bottom right reads '1090 / 0 / 11' clearly within a boxed total. Alignment is unambiguous and consistent with total formatting.
